SemiAnalysis.text:
**New this period:**
- The Electronic Design Automation (EDA) market is dominated by three companies: Synopsys, Cadence, and Siemens EDA, with over 85% combined market share.
- The EDA industry has posted positive revenue growth every year for over a decade, with a 13% CAGR, outpacing semiconductor R&D growth.
- EDA software tools represent roughly 9-12% of total semiconductor R&D spending, with this share expanding due to rising R&D intensity and verification costs.
- The Big-3 EDA vendors (Synopsys, Cadence, and Siemens EDA) generated combined revenue of around $16B in CY2025, with the broader EDA+IP industry totaling $18B.
- Synopsys acquired Ansys for $35B, expanding its addressable market to $31 billion.

**Details:**
**EDA Market Primer (https://newsletter.semianalysis.com/p/eda-market-primer)**
- The EDA market is led by Synopsys, Cadence, and Siemens EDA, with the Big-3 holding over 85% combined market share.
- Synopsys generated $8B in CY2025 (including Ansys), Cadence $5.30B, and Siemens EDA an estimated $2.2-2.5B.
- The EDA industry has grown at a 13% CAGR, outpacing semiconductor R&D growth, with this spread widening after 2018 due to hyperscaler AI chip programs and advanced node verification costs.
- EDA software tools represent roughly 9-12% of total semiconductor R&D spending, with this share expanding due to rising R&D intensity and verification costs.
- Key customers of EDA tools include fabless chip designers (NVIDIA, Qualcomm, AMD, Broadcom, MediaTek), systems companies (Google, Amazon, Microsoft, Meta, Apple, Tesla), IDMs (Intel, TI, Analog Devices, Infineon, STMicroelectronics), memory companies (Samsung, SK Hynix, Micron, Kioxia), foundries (TSMC, Samsung Foundry, Intel Foundry, GlobalFoundries, Rapidus), turnkey ASIC design houses (Broadcom ASIC Group, Marvell Custom Silicon, Alchip, GUC), and IP companies (ARM, Rambus, Alphawave).
- The EDA market is driven by four structural forces: node transitions, verification intensity, AI accelerator proliferation, and pricing power from lock-in.
- Synopsys, Cadence, and Siemens EDA have significant market shares in various EDA tool categories, including synthesis, place and route, signoff analysis, physical verification, functional simulation, and hardware emulation.
- Key metrics include: 
  - Synopsys's 84-85% share in synthesis tools
  - Cadence's 10-15pp gain in Place & Route market share between 2015-2020
  - Siemens's 85%+ share in physical verification
  - 65% of design time spent on verification
  - 4-7 months spent on implementation
  - 5% spent on physical verification
  - $18B total EDA market size (2025)
  - $28-30B projected EDA market size by 2030
- The EDA market is expected to continue growing, driven by increasing demand for advanced node verification, AI accelerator development, and rising R&D intensity.

Andrej Karpathy \u2013 Blog.text:
**New this period:**
- Andrej Karpathy releases a new art project called microgpt, a single file of 200 lines of pure Python that trains and infers a GPT model.
- The microgpt project simplifies large language models to their bare essentials and is available on GitHub and as a Google Colab notebook.
- The project uses a dataset of 32,000 names and a simple tokenizer that assigns one integer to each unique character in the dataset.

**Details:**
**[microgpt](http://karpathy.github.io/2026/02/12/microgpt/)**
- The core thesis of this piece is the release of microgpt, a simplified large language model implemented in a single Python file.
- Companies mentioned: GitHub, Google (for Google Colab notebook).
- The specific role of GitHub is to host the microgpt source code, and the specific role of Google is to provide the platform for the Google Colab notebook.
- Quantitative figures: 200 lines of code, 32,000 names in the dataset, 26 possible lowercase characters (a-z) in the tokenizer, and 1 special token (BOS) for a total vocabulary size of 27.
- Supply chain or value chain relationships: the project relies on the GitHub platform for hosting the code and the Google Colab notebook for interactive execution.
- Technology transitions: microgpt simplifies large language models by reducing them to their essential components, making it easier to understand and implement these models.
- The project uses a simple tokenizer and autograd engine, which are key components of large language models, and demonstrates their usage in a simplified setting.
- The autograd engine is implemented using a Value class that wraps scalar numbers and tracks how they were computed, allowing for automatic differentiation and gradient computation.
- No specific capital allocation signals or regulatory signals are mentioned in this piece.

- OpenAI has released GPT-5.5, a new family of models with significant improvements in coding and image generation capabilities.
- GPT-5.5 Pro is the most competent model in the family, accessible only on the website, and has demonstrated notable advancements in procedurally generating complex simulations.
- OpenAI's Codex, powered by GPT-5.5, can now generate near-PhD-quality academic papers and playable tabletop roleplaying games with illustrations.

**Details:**

- GPT-5.5 Pro completed a coding challenge in 20 minutes, whereas GPT-5.4 Pro took 33 minutes to complete the same task.
- The new image model, GPT-imagegen-2, can render high-quality text and create almost any picture described, including detailed art galleries.
- Supply chain and value chain relationships: OpenAI develops models, apps, and harnesses, while companies like Claude provide competing apps and models.
- Technology transitions: GPT-5.5 represents a significant improvement over previous models, with enhanced coding and image generation capabilities.
- Competitive positioning: OpenAI's advancements in models, apps, and harnesses position the company as a leader in the AI industry, with potential implications for competitors like Claude.
- Capital allocation signals: OpenAI's investments in model development, app creation, and harness improvements demonstrate the company's commitment to innovation and growth.
- Quantitative figures: GPT-5.5 Pro is 13 minutes faster than GPT-5.4 Pro in completing a coding challenge, and the new image model can generate detailed art galleries with readable labels.

Simon Willison.text:
**New this period:**
- FTC requires Cox Media Group and two other firms to pay nearly $1 million to settle charges of deceiving customers about an AI-powered marketing service.
- Datasette Agent, a new extensible AI assistant for Datasette, is announced with plugins for charts, image generation, and code execution.
- Datasette Agent plugins, including datasette-agent-charts, datasette-agent-openai-imagegen, and datasette-agent-sprites, are released.
- New releases of Datasette Agent and its plugins, including datasette-agent 0.1a3, datasette-agent-charts 0.1a2, and datasette-agent-sprites 0.1a0, are announced.

**Details:**
**FTC to Require Cox Media Group to Pay Nearly $1 Million (https://simonwillison.net/2026/May/22/ftc-active-listening/#atom-everything)**
- The FTC alleged that Cox Media Group, MindSift, and 1010 Digital Works deceived customers about their "Active Listening" AI-powered marketing service.
- Cox Media Group, MindSift, and 1010 Digital Works claimed their service listened to consumers' conversations in real-time to target advertising, but it actually resold email lists obtained from other data brokers.
- The companies claimed consumers had opted into the service by agreeing to terms of service, but the FTC stated that this did not constitute "adequate consent".
- The settlement requires the companies to pay nearly $1 million.
- Companies involved: Cox Media Group, MindSift, 1010 Digital Works.
- Quantitative figure: nearly $1 million.

**Datasette Agent (https://simonwillison.net/2026/May/21/datasette-agent/#atom-everything)**
- Datasette Agent is a new extensible AI assistant for Datasette.
- The agent provides a conversational interface for asking questions of data stored in Datasette.
- Plugins for Datasette Agent include datasette-agent-charts, datasette-agent-openai-imagegen, and datasette-agent-sprites.
- Companies/technologies involved: Datasette, Observable Plot, ChatGPT Images 2.0, Fly Sprites, Claude Code, OpenAI Codex.
- Quantitative figure: none.
- Technology transition: Datasette Agent introduces a new conversational interface for Datasette.

**Datasette Agent plugins (https://simonwillison.net/2026/May/21/datasette-agent-sprites/#atom-everything, https://simonwillison.net/2026/May/21/datasette-agent-charts/#atom-everything)**

Arxiv \u2013 Trading & Market Microstruc.text:
**New this period:**
- Researchers study the commitment problem in Maximal Extractable Value (MEV) auctions on the Ethereum blockchain, where block builders may defect from auction outcomes.
- The study finds that the cost of imperfect commitment depends on replicability and competition, and that MEV types such as sandwich opportunities are highly competitive, while others like naked arbitrage and liquidations leave more surplus exposed to builder defection.

**Details:**
**Imperfect Commitment in Maximal Extractable Value Auctions**(https://arxiv.org/abs/2605.22667)
- The research question is to study the commitment problem in MEV auctions, where Ethereum block builders may defect from auction outcomes with a probability of $\varepsilon$.
- The method involves modeling a builder who defects with probability $\varepsilon$ and replicates a type-specific fraction $\gamma(\tau)$ of the winning MEV opportunity, and using the \texttt{libmev} dataset to estimate $\gamma(\tau)$.
- Key findings include:
  - The cost of imperfect commitment depends jointly on replicability and competition.
  - The resulting equilibrium is piecewise.
  - There is sharp heterogeneity across MEV types, with sandwich opportunities being highly competitive, while naked arbitrage and liquidations leave substantially more surplus exposed to builder defection.
- Named companies or technologies: Ethereum
- Quantitative figures: 
  - $\varepsilon$ (probability of a builder defecting)
  - $\gamma(\tau)$ (type-specific fraction of the winning MEV opportunity replicated by a defecting builder)
- Supply chain or value chain relationships: 
  - Block builders run sealed auctions among searchers.
  - Searchers submit bundles to block builders.
- Technology transitions: 
  - The use of MEV auctions on the Ethereum blockchain.
- Competitive positioning: 
  - Builders who defect from auction outcomes may capture surplus, while searchers may choose between risky first-price bids and safe deterrence bids.

Arxiv \u2013 Computational Finance (q-fi.text:
**New this period:**
- Researchers introduce ThiopheneIV, a faster Black-Scholes implied-volatility solver with a monotone core.
- A model-free framework is developed for risk-neutral density extraction from short-dated option chains, using a pipeline consisting of arbitrage removal and smooth entropic density extraction.
- A study examines what deep hedging actually learns, finding that it often learns a systematic delta haircut relative to Black-Scholes, which can improve accumulated reward and terminal downside variance but is regime-fragile.
- A Generative Adversarial Graph Neural Network (Sig-Graph GAN) is introduced for generating synthetic time series data, outperforming baseline methods in replicating the distribution of logarithmic returns across different stock exchanges.

**Details:**
**Faster Monotone Implied Volatility Solver (https://arxiv.org/abs/2605.22427)**
- The paper presents ThiopheneIV, a Black-Scholes implied-volatility solver with a monotone core and explicit production guards.
- ThiopheneIV is compared to a Java port of J\"ackel's Let's Be Rational, and is found to be faster while keeping regular-grid errors close.
- The solver starts from the non-iterative Choi-Huh-Su L3 lower-bound seed and applies three Euler-Chebyshev corrections.
- The implementation includes parity normalisation, microscopic Bachelier-limit handling, saturated-price treatment, finite-update checks, fallback seeds, and an optional J\"ackel-Newton polish.

**From Arbitrage Removal to Density Extraction: A Model-Free Framework for Short-Dated Options (https://arxiv.org/abs/2605.22792)**
- The paper develops a model-free pipeline for risk-neutral density extraction from short-dated option chains, consisting of two steps: arbitrage removal and smooth entropic density extraction.
- The pipeline is tested on synthetic Heston panels and short-dated SPX option data, sampled from a few hours to one week before expiry.
- The pipeline returns robust densities across various market conditions, including scheduled macroeconomic announcements.
- The recovered densities are used to construct short-dated implied-volatility smiles.

**What Does Deep Hedging Actually Learn? Delta Corrections, Regime Fragility, and Symbolic Distillation (https://arxiv.org/abs/2605.21696)**
- The paper studies empirical deep hedging for S&P 500 index options under a local downside-shortfall reward.
- TD3 agents are compared with a daily-updated Black-Scholes delta hedge on the same option episodes.
- The agents learn a systematic delta haircut relative to Black-Scholes, which is explained by spot-implied-volatility co-movement.
- The correction improves accumulated reward and terminal downside variance, but is regime-fragile, with losses in adverse daily states in 2022 and underhedging raising ordinary variance in 2023.
- Symbolic regression distills the neural policies into compact formulas that preserve much of the reward, downside-variance, and CVaR advantage over Black-Scholes.

**A Generative Adversarial Graph Neural Network for Synthetic Time Series Data (https://arxiv.org/abs/2605.22215)**
- The paper introduces the Sig-Graph GAN model, which integrates the time-series signature, Long Short-Term Memory network, and Graph Neural Networks (GNNs) to generate synthetic time series data.
- The model uses the visibility graph algorithm to derive a graph-based representation of the underlying time series.
- Numerical evaluations demonstrate that the Sig-Graph GAN model outperforms baseline methods in replicating the distribution of logarithmic returns across different stock exchanges.
- The integration of the graph structure with the autoregressive component effectively captures both geometric and temporal patterns embedded in time-series data.

Arxiv \u2013 Portfolio Management (q-fin.text:
**New this period:**
- Researchers examine conditional allocation between growth/technology and defensive income/value-oriented ETF baskets using macro-market timing signals.
- A study models portfolio crossing as limited-communication preference elicitation over signed portfolio trades in institutional crossing markets.
- A paper tests whether graph neural networks improve realized volatility forecasts and portfolio performance using data from 465 S&P 500 equities.

**Details:**
**Continuous Timing Signals for Growth-Defensive Style Allocation (https://arxiv.org/abs/2605.20636)**
- The paper studies conditional allocation between a growth/technology ETF basket ($G$) and a defensive income/value-oriented ETF basket ($D$) using macro-market timing signals.
- The allocation framework combines rate relief, SPY drawdown depth, high-VIX stress relief, and a growth-crowding penalty to generate a continuous smooth score.
- The smooth-score policy uses a 50% maximum active tilt and obtains a 19.24% CAGR, a Sharpe ratio of 1.01, and a maximum drawdown of -31.63% from June 28, 2017 to May 15, 2026.
- The selected policy improves over 50/50 G/D, matched TNX-only, matched core-only, SPY, and volatility-matched 100% G benchmarks.
- Companies mentioned: None
- Quantitative figures: 0.273 market beta, -0.552 HML beta, 0.117 momentum beta, 1.95% annualized alpha, 0.81 Newey-West t-statistic, 19.24% CAGR, 1.01 Sharpe ratio, -31.63% maximum drawdown, 50% maximum active tilt, 10bp transaction costs

**Portfolio Preference Elicitation in Institutional Crossing Markets (https://arxiv.org/abs/2605.21409)**
- The paper models portfolio crossing as limited-communication preference elicitation over signed portfolio trades in institutional crossing markets.
- The analysis uses price-directed demand queries and verification queries to search the portfolio space and guide queries.
- Market-calibrated experiments using equity panels from the United States, Korea, Japan, and Germany show that the hybrid procedure recovers 88% of full-information welfare under a limited query budget.
- Companies mentioned: None
- Quantitative figures: 88% recovered welfare, 95% recovered welfare with expanded communication
- Supply chain/value chain relationships: Institutional crossing platforms use price-directed demand queries and verification queries to guide portfolio allocation

**Do Better Volatility Forecasts Lead to Better Portfolios? Evidence from Graph Neural Networks (https://arxiv.org/abs/2605.19278)**
- The paper tests whether graph neural networks improve realized volatility forecasts and portfolio performance using data from 465 S&P 500 equities.
- The empirical finding is that the model with the lowest forecast MSE, the model with the highest cross-sectional ranking accuracy, and the model with the highest portfolio Sharpe ratio are three different models.
- Companies mentioned: S&P 500
- Quantitative figures: 465 S&P 500 equities, 2015-2025 data period
- Technology transitions: Graph neural networks are used to improve realized volatility forecasts and portfolio performance.
